question_title: Will the interest in “kash patel” change between 2026-03-30 and 2026-04-09 according to Google Trends?
summary: Kash Patel's Google Trends interest spiked to 100 on Mar 28 (Iranian hack of his personal email) and has decayed to 37 by Mar 30. The resolution compares Mar 30 value (37) to Apr 9 value within the fixed date range Mar 10 - Apr 9. The Feb 23 spike precedent (94→8 over 12 days, decay rate ~0.886/day) projects the value to reach ~11 by Apr 9, well below the "Decreases" threshold of <34. While Patel remains highly newsworthy (hack follow-ups, Swalwell files, Iran tensions), the probability of a new event large enough AND timed near Apr 9 to keep values above 34 is low (~10-15%). "Decreases" is the dominant outcome.

factors.description: Post-spike decay pattern: Current value 37 is day-2 of a spike that peaked at 100 (Mar 28). The Feb 23 precedent spike (94) decayed from 32 on day 2 to ~8 by day 12. Exponential decay rate of 0.886/day projects value ~11 by Apr 9.
Active hack story with ongoing developments: FBI confirmed hack, $10M reward offered, Handala threatens more releases. This slows decay somewhat but is unlikely to prevent it over 10 days.
Swalwell files story running in parallel: Patel pushing to release decade-old FBI files on Swalwell/Fang. Could generate additional search interest but is a secondary thread.
Kash Patel is a highly newsworthy figure (FBI Director) with multiple ongoing controversies. P(new major event in next 10 days) ~30-40%, but needs to be timed near Apr 9 AND exceed threshold.
Threshold asymmetry at value 37: Post-spike decay direction overwhelmingly favors dropping below 34 (Decreases threshold) rather than rising above 40 (Increases threshold).
SerpAPI vs pytrends measurement uncertainty: Values may differ by 1-3 points. Creates small probability of Doesn't change if start value shifts.

reasoning:
**Forecast: "Decreases" at 78%**

Key reasoning:

- **Current situation**: Kash Patel's interest spiked to 100 on Mar 28 (Iranian-linked email hack) and has already decayed to 37 by Mar 30. The start value for comparison is 37.
- **Historical precedent**: The Feb 23 spike (94) followed a nearly identical decay pattern — by day 12, interest had fallen to ~8. The fitted exponential decay rate (0.886/day) projects Apr 9 value at ~11.
- **Threshold math**: "Decreases" requires end value < 34 (just 3 points below current). Standard post-spike decay crosses this threshold within 1-2 days. "Increases" requires > 40, and "Doesn't change" requires 34-40 — both need sustained or renewed interest.
- **Upside risk**: Patel remains highly newsworthy (hack follow-ups from Handala, Swalwell files, Iran tensions, potential hearings). A ~12% chance of a new major event coinciding with Apr 9 gives some probability to "Increases."

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|------------|
| Decreases | **78%** |
| Increases | **12%** |
| Doesn't change | **10%** |
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the interest in “kash patel” change between 2026-03-30 and 2026-04-09 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42980)

[Budget Hearing – Fiscal Year 2026 Request for the Federal Bureau of Investigation](https://appropriations.house.gov/schedule/hearings/budget-hearing-fiscal-year-2026-request-federal-bureau-investigation)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=kash%20patel&date=2026-03-10%202026-04-09](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=kash%20patel&date=2026-03-10%202026-04-09) for 2026-04-09 is within 3 of the value at 2026-03-30. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "kash patel",
  "date": "2026-03-10 2026-04-09",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
